draft_text: What are the signs and symptoms of psychosis?
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: The risk of violence and suicide decreases with treatment for psychosis, so it is important to seek help.
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: The National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH) has information on ways to get help and find a health care provider or access treatment .
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: Find tips to help prepare for and get the most out of your visit .

draft_text: For additional resources, including questions to ask your health care provider, visit the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality .

draft_text: A qualified psychologist, psychiatrist, or social worker can make a diagnosis and help develop a treatment plan.
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: There are several different types of antipsychotic medications, and they have different side effects, so it is important to work with a health care provider to determine the medication that is most effective with the fewest side effects.
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: Family support and education programs teach family members about psychosis as well as coping, communication, and problem-solving skills.
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: Family members who are informed and involved are more prepared to help loved ones through the recovery process.
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: Medication management (also called pharmacotherapy) means tailoring medication to a person’s specific needs by selecting the appropriate type and dose of medication to help reduce psychosis symptoms.
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: Supported employment and education services focus on return to work or school, using the support of a coach to help people achieve their goals.
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.

draft_text: Case management provides opportunities for people with psychosis to work with a case manager to address practical problems and improve access to needed support services.
rationale: Extracted from remote_understanding-psychosis_f5d72e519dc0.html and tagged for variants review.
